name: julia-pro
description: Master Julia 1.10+ with modern features, performance optimization,

Design and implement multiple dispatch type hierarchies for domain-specific problems
Optimize Julia code for performance using type stability, profiling, and BenchmarkTools.jl

Build scientific computing applications using DifferentialEquations.jl, Optimization.jl, and DataFrames.jl

promptTemplates.title: Basic Julia Question
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I [specific task] in Julia 1.10+? Please explain with a simple example.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner asking about basic Julia syntax or functionality

promptTemplates.title: Performance Optimization Request
promptTemplates.prompt: My Julia function [describe function] is slow. Please analyze and suggest optimizations for better performance.

promptTemplates.title: Type Hierarchy Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a multiple dispatch type hierarchy for [domain]. Include abstract types, concrete types, and example methods.

promptTemplates.title: Full Package Development
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete Julia package structure for [package name]. Include Project.toml setup, src files, test structure, and documentation outline.
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer creating a new Julia package

bestPractices: Prioritize type stability in all functions - use @code_warntype to verify
Use immutable structs by default unless mutation is specifically required
Format code consistently with JuliaFormatter.jl using BlueStyle
antiPatterns: Avoid type piracy - do not define methods for types you do not own
Avoid using global variables in performance-critical code
Avoid unnecessary allocations in hot loops

faq.question: What is multiple dispatch in Julia?
faq.answer: Multiple dispatch is Julia's key feature where function methods are selected based on the types of all arguments, not just the first one. This enables flexible and extensible code patterns.

faq.question: How do I achieve optimal performance in Julia?
faq.answer: Achieve optimal performance by maintaining type stability, avoiding global variables, using appropriate data structures, and profiling with Profile.jl and BenchmarkTools.jl.

faq.question: What is type stability in Julia?
faq.answer: Type stability means a function's return type can be inferred from input types alone. Use @code_warntype to check - concrete types in output indicate stability.

faq.question: How do I create a Julia package?
faq.answer: Use PkgTemplates.jl to generate a properly structured package with tests, documentation, and CI. Never edit Project.toml directly - use Pkg REPL or Pkg.jl API.

faq.question: What testing frameworks does Julia use?
faq.answer: Test.jl is the standard testing framework. Use TestSetExtensions for organized test sets and PropCheck.jl for property-based testing.

faq.question: How do I optimize memory allocations in Julia?
faq.answer: Reduce allocations by preallocating arrays, using views instead of slices, avoiding global variables, and leveraging static arrays when dimensions are known.
